title: $error
text: $error Returns the Uniface message number for the error. $error Return Values Uniface message number for the current field or entity data input error. Use Allowed only in error trigger. Allowed in all component types. Example: Using $error Depending on the error code, you may choose to implement functionality besides just providing a meaningful error message. For example, in the following code a form that lists all the valid codes is displayed if the user enters a code that is not allowed: trigger error variables vSelectedCode endvariables if ($error = 0126) message "Incorrect code. Select one from list." activate "CODES".exec(vSelectedCode) ; start other form if ($status = 1) ; user selected a code TYPECD = vSelectedCode ; copy selected value return (0) ; allow the user to continue else message "No code selected." return (-1) ; still wrong, so prevent leaving endif else message $text("%%$error") ;catch all other errors return (-1) endif end; trigger Copy Related Links $dberror Return the error code reported by the DBMS. $entname Return the name of the current entity or check for the existence of an entity in the component. trigger error System trigger that is activated when a predefined errors occurs.
